entity: Working in the Time of COVID-19 Oral History Project
explicit_text: I participated in the Working in the Time of COVID-19 Oral History Project on December 27, 2021, sharing my experiences as an essential worker during the pandemic. The interview was conducted remotely with Wendi Zhou, who was in Miami while I was in Seattle. It was important for me to talk about how COVID-19 affected my work and daily life, especially working at Puget Consumers Co-op. This project gave me a chance to reflect on the challenges and changes I faced during this time.
implicit_text: I participated in an oral history project on December 27, 2021, sharing my experiences as an essential worker during the pandemic. The interview was conducted remotely with Wendi Zhou, who was in Miami while I was in Seattle. It was important for me to talk about how COVID-19 affected my work and daily life, especially working at a local cooperative grocery store. This project gave me a chance to reflect on the challenges and changes I faced during this time.

explicit_text: During my oral history interview for the Working in the Time of COVID-19 Oral History Project, Wendi Zhou was in Miami, Florida, while I was in Seattle. Although I haven't lived there, Miami was the location of the interviewer, which made our conversation remote. It was interesting to connect across such a distance during the pandemic. Miami represented the other side of the country during our discussion about my experiences.

That Florida city represented the other side of the country during our discussion about my experiences.

explicit_text: Florida is where the interviewer, Wendi Zhou, was located during our remote interview for the Working in the Time of COVID-19 Oral History Project. I live in Seattle, Washington, so Florida was far from my home, but it was part of the context of our conversation. The pandemic made remote interviews like this necessary, connecting people from different states. Florida was important as the interview setting on the other end of the call.

explicit_text: I currently live in Seattle, Washington, specifically in the Beacon Hill neighborhood. Seattle has been my home during the COVID-19 pandemic, where I worked at Puget Consumers Co-op and also did packaging support at the University of Washington bookstore. Living here shaped my experience as an essential worker, especially given how the pandemic affected the city. Seattle was where I navigated all the challenges of working and staying safe during this time.
implicit_text: I currently live in a major Pacific Northwest city, specifically in the Beacon Hill neighborhood. This city has been my home during the COVID-19 pandemic, where I worked at a cooperative grocery store and also did packaging support at a large public university bookstore. Living here shaped my experience as an essential worker, especially given how the pandemic affected the city. This city was where I navigated all the challenges of working and staying safe during this time.

explicit_text: I was born in Zimbabwe, Africa, on January 1, 1998. Although I now live in Seattle, my birthplace is an important part of my identity. Growing up with that background has influenced how I see myself, even as I explore my Latina heritage. Zimbabwe is where my life began before I moved to the United States.

Growing up with that background has influenced how I see myself, even as I explore my Latina heritage. That African country is where my life began before I moved to the United States.

explicit_text: I live in the Beacon Hill neighborhood of Seattle, Washington. This is where I have been based during the COVID-19 pandemic while working various jobs including at Puget Consumers Co-op. Beacon Hill is my home and the community I return to after long, exhausting shifts. It’s the place where I manage my daily life and safety precautions amid the pandemic.
implicit_text: I live in the Beacon Hill neighborhood of a major city in Washington state. This is where I have been based during the COVID-19 pandemic while working various jobs including at a cooperative grocery store. Beacon Hill is my home and the community I return to after long, exhausting shifts. It’s the place where I manage my daily life and safety precautions amid the pandemic.

explicit_text: I worked at the University of Washington bookstore packaging books during the pandemic. This was one of my side gigs alongside catering and working at Puget Consumers Co-op. The University of Washington is a major institution in Seattle, and working there during COVID-19 meant adapting to new safety protocols. It was part of how I managed to keep working during uncertain times.
implicit_text: I worked at a large public university's bookstore packaging books during the pandemic. This was one of my side gigs alongside catering and working at a cooperative grocery store. That university is a major institution in Seattle, and working there during COVID-19 meant adapting to new safety protocols. It was part of how I managed to keep working during uncertain times.

explicit_text: I live and work in Washington state, specifically Seattle, which was one of the early hotspots for COVID-19 in the U.S. The governor’s stay home stay healthy order designated me as an essential worker because of my job at Puget Consumers Co-op. Washington’s response to the pandemic shaped my work experience, including the challenges of safety and hazard pay. Living here during the pandemic deeply influenced my daily life and work conditions.
implicit_text: I live and work in the state of Washington, specifically Seattle, which was one of the early hotspots for COVID-19 in the U.S. The governor’s stay home stay healthy order designated me as an essential worker because of my job at a cooperative grocery store. The state’s response to the pandemic shaped my work experience, including the challenges of safety and hazard pay. Living here during the pandemic deeply influenced my daily life and work conditions.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic drastically changed my life and work starting in early 2020. As an essential worker at Puget Consumers Co-op in Seattle, I had to navigate new safety protocols like mask-wearing and sanitizing, which made my work more stressful and exhausting. The pandemic also affected my mental health, making me more self-conscious and cautious. It was a time when grocery workers were suddenly seen as heroes, but the reality was intense and challenging for me.
implicit_text: The global pandemic drastically changed my life and work starting in early 2020. As an essential worker at a cooperative grocery store in Seattle, I had to navigate new safety protocols like mask-wearing and sanitizing, which made my work more stressful and exhausting. The pandemic also affected my mental health, making me more self-conscious and cautious. It was a time when grocery workers were suddenly seen as heroes, but the reality was intense and challenging for me.

explicit_text: During the COVID-19 pandemic, I worked at Puget Consumers Co-op (PCC) in Seattle as a deli worker. PCC designated me as an essential worker, and I experienced firsthand the increased workload and safety challenges during the pandemic. We had to fight for hazard pay, which was eventually granted after pressure from customers and union support. Working at PCC was exhausting but also made me feel part of a vital frontline workforce during a difficult time.
